ActualValue:
(Number of First Call Resolutions / : 8380
Total Number of Inbound Calls attended) : 16604 =50.47%
Justification: In the last quarter, we transitioned from the Odoo system to the new iDesk platform. While the shift initially caused some delays in complaint resolution and an increase in repeated calls—contributing to a temporary drop in First Call Resolution it was a necessary step toward improving overall system performance. The new platform is designed to streamline internal workflows, unify all customer service channels, and enhance long-term service efficiency.
ActionPlan: The new system will be in place as we are waiting IA approval for iDesk. Enhancements are still ongoing, and we expect a significant improvement in overall performance, along with faster complaint resolution as well as high FCR ratio across all service channels.
sourceFile: Customer Care & Claims & CX\Customer Care\Copy of Q2-25 Customer Care  QA KRI Tree 2.xlsx

ActualValue:
(Number of Abandon calls : 1561 
Total Number of Inbound Calls) : 18258= 8.55%
Justification: The call answer time was set to 3 seconds, which significantly contributed to the increase in the call abandonment rate. A major factor behind this spike is the transition to the new iDesk system, which also included migrating the call center system.
ActionPlan: The call answer time has been increased from 3 seconds to 10 seconds to give agents sufficient time to respond to incoming calls. Additionally, the new system will be in place, with several enhancements  to further improve performance and efficiency.
sourceFile: Customer Care & Claims & CX\Customer Care\Copy of Q2-25 Customer Care  QA KRI Tree 2.xlsx

Justification: In the last quarter, we transitioned from the Odoo  to the new iDesk platform. ActionPlan: The new system will be in place as we are waiting IA approval for iDesk and we’re already seeing positive results. Enhancements are still ongoing, and we expect a significant improvement in overall performance, along with faster complaint resolution across all service channels.

KRITitle: Delay in compliant closure - Insurance Authority
ActualValue: Count of delayed closure complaints of Insurance Authority complaints :72 Count of total IA closed complaints) *: 476 =15.13%
Justification:
The spike in delayed IA complaints last quarter was mainly due to the IA team’s transition to a new system. This shift caused delays in complaint resolution, driven by multiple factors—most notably, system crashes, agent access issues, and the need for IA to manually create usernames and passwords for all complaint handlers.
Also, the claims backlog of Q1 impacted on complaint resolution as some of complaint been closed in Q2.
ActionPlan: The issue was raised with the IA team, and after several back-and-forth meetings, it has been resolved. The IA portal is now stable and fully operational.

KRITitle: Delay in compliant closure - Insurance Authority
ActualValue: Count of delayed closure complaints of Insurance Authority complaints :8 Count of total IA closed complaints) *100: 548 =1.45%
Justification: The issue is due to technical errors, including delays in policy issuance and delays in linking medical policies. This is primarily caused by the lack of verification of the member count prior to policy issuance.
ActionPlan: A daily report is received from the Technical Department highlighting unissued policies and the related reasons for follow-up. In addition, access to Tawuniya’s admin policy system has been requested to avoid delays and allow direct handling without depending on Tawuniya. The request has been approved, and access is currently in progress.

ActualValue:
Count of delayed closure complaints : 35
Count of total closed complaints) *: 821 =4.85%
Justification:
The increase in delayed cases was mainly impacted by a technical issue related to medical insurance policies. This issue affected the processing and closure of internal complaints.
As a result of this technical problem, some complaints were not closed within the regulatory timeline of 5 days, leading to a temporary increase in the delay figures.
ActionPlan:
The root cause of the issue was identified during the quotation process, where customers were provided with quotations without verifying the base number with CHI.

Additionally, a technical issue was identified in domestic workers’ medical insurance, which caused policies not to be issued due to a system error.

Another technical issue was also identified in domestic workers’ contract insurance, where cancellations could not be processed manually. As a corrective action, an automation solution was implemented to process cancellations directly through the Musaned platform.

All identified issues were addressed, and the necessary fixes were implemented by the end of December, to ensure smoother processing and prevent recurrence.
sourceFile: Customer Care & Claims & CX\Customer Care\Q4-2025 Customer Care KRIs.xlsx

KRITitle: Delay in compliant closure - Insurance Authority
ActualValue: Count of delayed closure complaints of Insurance Authority complaints :8 Count of total IA closed complaints) *100: 635 =1.25%
Justification:
The increase in delayed cases was mainly impacted by a technical issue related to medical insurance policies. This issue affected the processing and closure of internal complaints.
As a result of this technical problem, some complaints were not closed within the regulatory timeline of 5 days, leading to a temporary increase in the delay figures.
ActionPlan:
The root cause of the issue was identified during the quotation process, where customers were provided with quotations without verifying the base number with CHI.

Additionally, a technical issue was identified in domestic workers’ medical insurance, which caused policies not to be issued due to a system error.

Another technical issue was also identified in domestic workers’ contract insurance, where cancellations could not be processed manually. As a corrective action, an automation solution was implemented to process cancellations directly through the Musaned platform.

All identified issues were addressed, and the necessary fixes were implemented by the end of December, to ensure smoother processing and prevent recurrence.
sourceFile: Customer Care & Claims & CX\Customer Care\Q4-2025 Customer Care KRIs.xlsx

Justification:
The delay was mainly caused by technical issues related to medical insurance policies, including domestic workers’ medical insurance, where policies were not issued due to system errors.

In addition, a technical issue was identified in domestic workers’ contract insurance, where policy cancellations could not be processed as required. This resulted in delays in complaint closure.

These issues are internal system-related matters and differ from complaints registered with the Authority. The combined impact of these technical challenges led to an increase in the number of complaints not closed within the regulatory timeline of 5 days.
ActionPlan:
The root cause of the issue was identified during the quotation process, where customers were provided with quotations without verifying the base number with CHI.

Additionally, a technical issue was identified in domestic workers’ medical insurance, which caused policies not to be issued due to a system error.

Another technical issue was also identified in domestic workers’ contract insurance, where cancellations could not be processed manually. As a corrective action, an automation solution was implemented to process cancellations directly through the Musaned platform.

All identified issues were addressed, and the necessary fixes were implemented by the end of December, to ensure smoother processing and prevent recurrence.
sourceFile: Customer Care & Claims & CX\Customer Care\Q4-2025 Customer Care KRIs.xlsx

Justification:
In Q1 we ran 3 Experiments and all 3 were successfull
1st Running traffic on social increased traffic and led higher number of  leads
2nd Running multiple bid strategies on comprehensive motor increased sales and ROAS 
3rd. Running different style of assets on social as an A/b test led to better understanding of the market interest

Also, we ran 2 Experiments and they were unssuccessfull
1st testing IH camapigns based on intent and behavior level was not successful 
2nd Targeting DWH policy holders for Home insurance product -- consented target was very small (30 users)

Comments:
As discussed, Marketing team suggested to change the threshold to the following
 >100%
<=90% – >=100%
<90%
and change the equation to
 benchmark / Achived CAC
